content:
## Post Content

I have an alpha that shows a relatively low IC, but the Sharpe ratio remains acceptable over a long simulation period. This seems counterintuitive to me, as I expected IC and Sharpe to move more closely together.

I suspect the behavior may be related to diversification across the universe or the holding period smoothing out noise. I would like to understand under what conditions this situation is reasonable and when it should be a warning sign.
